mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, exerting direct downward pressure on S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL exceeding 205.0 signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, creating a negative return environment for equities over the medium term.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns are observed in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 205.0.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of peak housing starts (HOUST > 1500) and restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 5.0) creates a structural vulnerability in the housing sector, leading to negative equity returns as the bubble bursts.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns are observed in > 50% of instances where both HOUST > 1500 and FEDFUNDS > 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: High non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate robust labor market conditions and consumer spending power, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive equity returns over the short term.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns are observed in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 137000.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong economic activity and capacity utilization, driving positive equity returns, but this relationship holds only during expansionary phases and breaks down during recessions.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns are observed in > 55% of instances where INDPRO > 110 during confirmed expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in retail sales (RSAFS) below 360,000 signals weakening consumer demand, leading to immediate negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are revised downward in the short term.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns are observed in > 60% of instances where RSAFS < 360000.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5) and decline in real GDP (GDPC1 < 11500) confirms a recessionary onset, causing significant negative equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns are observed in > 50% of instances where both UNRATE > 4.5 and GDPC1 < 11500.

mechanism_narrative: High real interest rates, proxied by the combination of high nominal rates (FEDFUNDS > 5.0) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 205), increase the cost of capital, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns are observed in > 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 5.0 and CPIAUCSL > 205.
